purpose: Categorizes the current AI capex cycle as a self-funded bust distinct from debt-funded busts to predict correction dynamics of slow grind rather than crash
components: Analogue 1: Japanese corporates 1990s-2000s — TOPIX underperformed ~60% over 13 years
Analogue 2: Energy majors 2010-2014 — P/E compressed 14x to 8x over 2 years
No creditor-imposed stop-loss; discipline is internal or shareholder-driven
Behavioral model: 12-18 months tolerance then 2-quarter compressed capitulation
Phase transition indicators: capex/revenue stalling 3+ quarters, sequential AI revenue decline, management language shift
current_assessment: Current cycle maps to early-to-mid-cycle by both analogues. No phase transition indicators active. Modal outcome is 5-10% annual underperformance compounding over years rather than sharp drawdown. Unresolved whether capital flows create non-linearity absent in analogues.
limitations: N=2 for self-funded bust analogues is thin
Japan had home bias and energy majors had no FX feedback; current cycle has both
US shareholder activism may provide discipline absent in Japanese analogues
Cannot determine slow grind vs sharp correction until initial repricing reveals capital flow persistence
